questions.q: Which Mughal emperor built Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri was built by the emperor Akbar in the 1570s.

questions.q: Fatehpur Sikri was built near which major Mughal city?
questions.explanation: The planned city of Fatehpur Sikri lies near Agra in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: What purpose was Fatehpur Sikri built to serve?
questions.explanation: Akbar built the city as his imperial capital, though it served that role only briefly.

questions.q: The colossal gateway of Fatehpur Sikri's mosque is known as the?
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza, or Gate of Magnificence, is the towering entrance to the Jama Masjid at Fatehpur Sikri.

questions.q: What grand place of worship stands at Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri's Jama Masjid is one of the city's most renowned structures.

questions.q: Fatehpur Sikri blends Indian style with which other architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: The city's architecture combines Persian and Indian elements in a distinctive Mughal synthesis.

questions.q: What is the most commonly cited reason Fatehpur Sikri was abandoned?
questions.explanation: The city was abandoned after a short time, reportedly because of a shortage of water.

questions.q: In which decade did Akbar build Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Akbar built the city in the 1570s during the middle of his reign.

questions.q: What does the name 'Fatehpur' broadly signify?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur means 'city of victory', linked to Akbar's military successes.

questions.q: Most of Fatehpur Sikri's buildings are constructed from which material?
questions.explanation: The city is built largely of red sandstone, giving it a unified reddish appearance.

questions.q: Akbar is said to have founded Fatehpur Sikri to honour which Sufi saint?
questions.explanation: Akbar built the city near the dwelling of Shaikh Salim Chishti, whose blessing he credited for the birth of his heir.

questions.q: The birth of which future emperor was linked to the saint's blessing that inspired the city?
questions.explanation: Akbar's son Salim, the future Jahangir, was named after Shaikh Salim Chishti.

questions.q: The white marble tomb within the complex is the dargah of whom?
questions.explanation: The delicate marble tomb at Fatehpur Sikri is the dargah of the Sufi saint Salim Chishti.

questions.q: The Buland Darwaza was added to commemorate Akbar's conquest of which region?
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza was raised to mark Akbar's victory in the Gujarat campaign.

questions.q: Which hall at Fatehpur Sikri is famed for its central pillar with a circular platform from which Akbar held court?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Khas features a richly carved central pillar supporting a platform linked by stone bridges.

questions.q: Akbar's house for interfaith religious debates at Fatehpur Sikri was the?
questions.explanation: The Ibadat Khana, or House of Worship, hosted Akbar's famous discussions among scholars of different faiths.

questions.q: Which five-storeyed open pavilion at Fatehpur Sikri is named for its tiered design?
questions.explanation: The Panch Mahal is a five-tiered columned pavilion, each storey smaller than the one below.

questions.q: Fatehpur Sikri has been recognised since 1986 as what?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1986.

questions.q: After Akbar left Fatehpur Sikri, to which city did the Mughal capital effectively return?
questions.explanation: Following the abandonment, the court returned to Agra and Akbar later moved operations toward Lahore.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Fatehpur Sikri serve as Akbar's principal capital before being largely abandoned?
questions.explanation: The city functioned as Akbar's main capital for roughly fourteen years before being abandoned in the mid-1580s.

questions.q: The ornamental water tank at the heart of the palace complex, near a small island platform, is called the?
questions.explanation: The Anup Talao is a square ornamental pool with a central platform reached by causeways.

questions.q: Fatehpur Sikri is built on a low ridge of which type of rock outcrop?
questions.explanation: The city stands on a sandstone ridge near Sikri, the source of its red building stone.

questions.q: In which year is the founding of Fatehpur Sikri as Akbar's capital generally dated?
questions.explanation: Akbar began building Fatehpur Sikri around 1571 after the auspicious birth of his heir.

questions.q: An inscription on the Buland Darwaza quotes a famous saying attributed to which figure, advising that the world is a bridge?
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza bears an inscription of a saying attributed to Jesus: 'The world is a bridge; pass over it but build no house upon it.'

questions.q: The palace popularly called 'Jodha Bai's Palace' actually housed whom in scholarly understanding?
questions.explanation: The large palace traditionally named for Jodha Bai is understood to be a principal residence of Akbar's harem and senior queens.

questions.q: Which courtyard game is said to have been played at Fatehpur Sikri using a marked stone board and human pieces?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Akbar played pachisi on a large courtyard using slave girls as living pieces.

questions.q: The Buland Darwaza is often cited as among the tallest gateways of its kind, standing at roughly what height?
questions.explanation: The Buland Darwaza rises about 54 metres including its base, making it one of the highest gateways in the world.

questions.q: Besides water scarcity, Fatehpur Sikri's abandonment is also linked to Akbar's need to campaign in which direction?
options: Bengal in the far east only
questions.explanation: Akbar shifted his base toward Lahore in the mid-1580s to deal with affairs on the northwest frontier, hastening the city's abandonment.

questions.q: The Diwan-i-Khas pillar's design is notable for combining motifs drawn from several religious traditions, reflecting which policy of Akbar?
questions.explanation: The eclectic carved bracket capitals of the Diwan-i-Khas reflect Akbar's syncretic, tolerant religious vision.

questions.q: The small but elaborately carved building at Fatehpur Sikri often identified as the residence or office of a court favourite is popularly called the house of whom?
questions.explanation: A finely carved residence at Fatehpur Sikri is popularly known as the House of Birbal, after Akbar's celebrated courtier.
